days.title: Day 1: Begin gentle motion
days.focus: Reduce fear and start easy finger movement.
days.summary: Today is about noticing symptoms and beginning short, gentle motion without forcing the joint.
normalSignals: Light swelling that settles with rest
safetyNotes: Stop if symptoms worsen and contact a clinician for severe pain, numbness, color change, fever, pus, sudden swelling, or inability to move.

days.title: Day 2: Repeat small ranges
days.focus: Keep the motion calm and repeatable.
days.summary: Use the same gentle range as yesterday and avoid testing the finger with force.
normalSignals: Stiffness that eases after a few gentle reps
safetyNotes: Follow clinician instructions first and stop if pain escalates instead of settling.

days.title: Day 3: Add light glide practice
days.focus: Introduce slow tendon glides without gripping hard.
days.summary: A short glide sequence can help you practice controlled movement while staying inside a comfortable range.
safetyNotes: Do not force a fist; contact a clinician for severe pain, numbness, color change, fever, pus, sudden swelling, or inability to move.

days.title: Day 4: Build consistency
days.focus: Practice gentle motion at a steady pace.
days.summary: Today keeps the same safe movements and emphasizes consistency over range.
normalSignals: Motion feels easier after warming up
getHelpSignals: Finger color becomes blue or purple
safetyNotes: Keep reps gentle and stop if symptoms worsen or do not match your clinician's expectations.

days.title: Day 5: Check daily-use comfort
days.focus: Connect gentle movement to light daily tasks.
days.summary: Use the exercises to prepare for light daily use, not heavy gripping or resistance.
safetyNotes: Avoid heavy gripping and contact a clinician if severe or unusual symptoms appear.

days.title: Day 6: Keep motion smooth
days.focus: Make each rep slow and controlled.
days.summary: Smooth motion matters more than reaching the full range today.
normalSignals: The finger feels stiff at first
safetyNotes: Do not force through sharp pain; stop and seek clinician guidance if symptoms worsen.

days.title: Day 7: Review the first week
days.focus: Notice what is easier and what still needs caution.
days.summary: End the first week by repeating the gentle routine and checking for warning signs.
safetyNotes: If symptoms are worsening rather than gradually settling, contact a clinician before continuing.

days.title: Day 8: Add light control
days.focus: Begin very light control work without resistance.
days.summary: If gentle motion has stayed comfortable, add small tabletop lifts while keeping the effort easy.
safetyNotes: Skip strengthening practice if your clinician told you to avoid it or symptoms worsen.

days.title: Day 9: Practice light touch
days.focus: Use gentle contact without hard gripping.
days.summary: Today connects movement with careful touch practice while avoiding force.
safetyNotes: Do not squeeze hard; stop and contact a clinician for severe pain, numbness, color change, fever, pus, sudden swelling, or inability to move.

days.title: Day 10: Keep effort low
days.focus: Repeat light control with relaxed effort.
days.summary: The goal is controlled movement, not strength testing.
getHelpSignals: Pain that does not settle with rest
safetyNotes: Avoid weights, resistance bands, or strong gripping in this first-version plan.

days.title: Day 11: Combine motion and touch
days.focus: Use a short sequence that stays gentle.
days.summary: Combine bending, gliding, and light touch so the hand practices a safe routine.
safetyNotes: Stop and contact a clinician if warning signs appear or your symptoms feel outside your expected recovery instructions.

days.title: Day 12: Support daily confidence
days.focus: Prepare for light daily use with caution.
days.summary: Use the routine to build confidence for simple tasks while avoiding heavy or painful use.
normalSignals: The finger may still feel slower than the other side
safetyNotes: Do not treat this plan as clearance for heavy work or sport; follow clinician instructions.

days.title: Day 13: Review progress safely
days.focus: Notice progress without forcing the final range.
days.summary: Repeat the routine and compare comfort, confidence, and warning signs rather than chasing perfect motion.
safetyNotes: Contact a clinician if symptoms are worsening, severe, or accompanied by concerning signs.

days.title: Day 14: Finish the starter plan
days.focus: Complete the two-week starter routine and decide what needs clinician follow-up.
days.summary: Today closes the starter plan with a calm review of what improved and what still needs professional guidance.
safetyNotes: This plan does not replace a follow-up visit. Contact a clinician for severe pain, numbness, color change, fever, pus, sudden swelling, inability to move, or concerns that do not improve.
